prerequisites: An Apify API token, available on the Integrations page in the Apify Console (https://console.apify.com/settings/integrations).
The dataset_id of the Apify dataset to read, either the dataset ID or a username~dataset-name identifier.

authMethods.name: API token
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with your Apify API token, supplied via the connector's secret `token` field. Apify accepts the token either as an `Authorization: Bearer <token>` request header (recommended, since URLs are often stored in browser history and server logs) or as a `?token=` query parameter. You can find or create your token on the Integrations page in the Apify Console.

setupSteps.title: Get your Apify API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Apify Console and open the Integrations page at https://console.apify.com/settings/integrations. Copy your existing API token, or add a new one. Treat the token as a secret and never share it with untrusted parties.

setupSteps.title: Find the dataset ID
setupSteps.body: Identify the dataset you want to read. The connector's `dataset_id` field accepts either the dataset ID (for example WkzbQMuFYuamGv3YF) or a `username~dataset-name` identifier. Dataset items are served from GET https://api.apify.com/v2/datasets/:datasetId/items.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the required `dataset_id` and paste your API token into the required secret `token` field. The connector uses the token to authenticate requests against the Apify API v2 and retrieve the dataset's items.
